dwarf.welcome: Welcome new Dwarf leader! Dwarves are specialized in industrial production and also have a strong defensive advantage. This tutorial will show you the steps to get started with your empire.
dwarf.land: Every empire needs as much land as they can get. While under new player protection, your only option to get more land is exploring. Click Explore in the sidebar.
dwarf.explore: Explore for around 100 turns to get a base amount of land.
dwarf.build: Now that you have land, you need to build buildings. Click Build in the sidebar.
dwarf.strategy: As a Dwarf, your goal will be to produce as many troops as possible and sell the excess on the Public Market. To do this, you will need to have your land divided by around 85% blacksmiths, 10% farms, and 5% mage towers. Mage towers will bring wizards to your land that will provide you with magical defense and the ability to change eras. Build blacksmiths, farms, and mage towers until all of your land is used.
dwarf.production: Now you should have a good base of troop production. If you find yourself running low on cash, you can sell troops immediately on the Black Market. Now we will start making troops. Click Industry in the sidebar.
dwarf.industry: Industry in batches of 25 turns and note the troops you are producing and the expenses you are spending. You can customize which troops are being produced by setting the percentages below.
dwarf.market: You should now have troops sell on the Public Market. You may want to keep some troops around for attack and defense purposes. Click Public Market in the sidebar.
dwarf.pricing: Take note of the price of troops if any other players are selling troops as well. Click the Sell tab.
dwarf.selling: Set a competitive price for your troops and enter how much you want to sell of each type then click Sell Goods.
dwarf.waiting: Your troops will now be sent to the Public Market for other players to buy. They will arrive in 6 hours. After 12 hours you can edit the price or recall the items for a penalty. If another player buys your troops, the money will be sent to your bank account. Click the arrow to advance.
dwarf.magic: Now it is time to protect yourself from magic attacks. Click Magic Center in the sidebar.
dwarf.shield: Check your magic power and mana supply. If you have a high enough magic power and enough mana, cast a Spell Shield. This will protect you from magical attacks for 24 hours. If you do not have enough magic power, you must use more turns to bring more wizards to your empire.
dwarf.era: As a Dwarf, you are best suited for the Future Era. You can change eras by using the Advance to Present spell and then the Advance to Future Spell. Be sure you have enough magic power and mana to cast these spells. It may take a while to build up the resources needed to change eras. You must also wait 72 hours after changing eras to change again.
dwarf.conclusion: You have now seen the basics of an industrial Dwarf Empire. Your turns are best used gaining land, building blacksmiths, and selling extra troops. Use the money you make to support your army's expenses and food consumption. Once you have exited new player protection, go to the War Council for a tutorial on attacking other players.

elf.welcome: Welcome new Elf leader! Elves are specialized in magic. This tutorial will show you the steps to get started with your empire.
elf.land: Every empire needs as much land as they can get. While under new player protection, your only option to get more land is exploring. Click Explore in the sidebar.
elf.explore: Explore for around 100 turns to get a base amount of land.
elf.build: Now that you have land, you need to build buildings. Click Build in the sidebar.
elf.strategy: As an Elf, your goal will be to produce as much money as you can by casting spells. To do this, you will need to have your land divided by around 90% mage towers and 10% farms. Mage towers will bring wizards to your land that can cast spells and generate mana. Wizards like to eat a lot so farms can help offset that.
elf.mana: Now you will have wizards come to your empire and generate mana. If you find yourself running low on food, you can buy food immediately on the Black Market but prices are usually better on the Public Market. Now we will start making Mana. Click Meditate in the sidebar.
elf.meditate: Meditate in batches of 25 turns and note the mana you are producing and the wizards coming in to your empire. It will take many turns for your mage towers to fill up with wizards. Click the arrow to continue the tutorial.
elf.magic: Now lets see about casting spells. Click Magic Center in the sidebar.
elf.spells: Check your magic power and mana supply. If you have a high enough magic power and enough mana, cast Tree of Gold 10 times (20 turns) to create money. If you do not have enough magic power, you must use more turns to bring more wizards to your empire. Next cast Cornucopia 5 times (10 turns) to create food. Click the arrow to continue the tutorial.
elf.shield: You may also want to defend yourself from magic attacks. Cast a spell shield to protect yourself from magical attacks for 24 hours. Click the arrow to continue the tutorial.
elf.conclusion: You should now continue to grow your empire through exploring and attacking other players. Build more mage towers as you go to increase the effectiveness of your spells. Use the money you make to buy an army of troops from either of the markets. Once you have exited new player protection, go to the War Council for a tutorial on attacking other players.

hobbit.land: Every empire needs as much land as they can get. While under new player protection, your only option to get more land is exploring. Click Explore in the sidebar.
hobbit.explore: Explore for around 100 turns to get a base amount of land.

hobbit.production: Now you should have a good base of food production. If you find yourself running low on cash, you can sell food immediately on the Black Market. Now we will start farming. Click Farm in the sidebar.
hobbit.farming: Farm in batches of 25 turns and note the food you are producing and the expenses you are spending.
hobbit.market: You should now have excess food available to sell on the Public Market. Click Public Market in the sidebar.
hobbit.pricing: Take note of the price of food if any other players are selling food as well. Click the Sell tab.
hobbit.selling: Set a competitive price for your food and enter how much you want to sell then click Sell Goods.
hobbit.waiting: Your food will now be sent to the Public Market for other players to buy. It will arrive in 6 hours. After 12 hours you can edit the price or recall the items for a penalty. If another player buys your food, the money will be sent to your bank account. Click the arrow to advance.

hobbit.shield: Check your magic power and mana supply. If you have a high enough magic power and enough mana, cast a Spell Shield. This will protect you from magical attacks for 24 hours. If you do not have enough magic power, you must use more turns to bring more wizards to your empire.
hobbit.era: As a Hobbit, you are best suited for the Present Era. You can change eras by using the Advance to Present spell. Be sure you have enough magic power and mana to cast the spell. It may take a while to build up the resources needed to change eras.
hobbit.conclusion: You have now seen the basics of a farming Hobbit Empire. Your turns are best used gaining land, building farms, and selling food. Use the money you make to buy an army of troops. Once you have exited new player protection, go to the War Council for a tutorial on attacking other players.

gnome.welcome: Welcome new Gnome leader! Gnomes are specialized in economic production and also have a strong Black Market advantage. This tutorial will show you the steps to get started with your empire.
gnome.land: Every empire needs as much land as they can get. While under new player protection, your only option to get more land is exploring. Click Explore in the sidebar.
gnome.explore: Explore for around 100 turns to get a base amount of land.
gnome.build: Now that you have land, you need to build buildings. Click Build in the sidebar.
gnome.strategy: As a gnome, your goal will be to produce as much money as possible and buy and sell on the Black and Public Markets. To do this, you will need to have your land divided by around 45% Huts, 45% markets and 10% mage towers. Mage towers will bring wizards to your land that will provide you with magical defense and the ability to change eras. Build huts, markets, and mage towers until all of your land is used.
gnome.production: Now you should have a good base of cash production. If you find yourself running low on food, you can buy food immediately on the Black Market but prices are usually better on the Public Market. Now we will start making more cash. Click Cash in the sidebar.
gnome.cashing: Cash in batches of 25 turns and note the money you are producing and the expenses you are spending. You can take a more advanced approach to cashing by manipulating your tax rate. It is beneficial to lower your tax rate to 1% for a few turns to bring peasants into your empire, and then raise your tax rate to 100% for a few turns to get the most money out of them. Experiment with different tax rates to find the best balance for your empire.
gnome.banking: You should now have an excess of cash available in your empire. Click The Bank in the sidebar.
gnome.market: Deposit money in the Bank to earn interest. You can also take out loans if you need some quick cash. Now its time to buy things from the markets. Click Black Market in the sidebar.
gnome.blackmarket: Take note of the prices of items available on the Black Market. Click Public Market in the sidebar.
gnome.publicmarket: Take note of the prices of items available on the Public Market. Compare between the two to find the best deals. Click the arrow to continue the tutorial
gnome.magic: Now it is time to protect yourself from magic attacks. Click Magic Center in the sidebar.
gnome.shield: Check your magic power and mana supply. If you have a high enough magic power and enough mana, cast a Spell Shield. This will protect you from magical attacks for 24 hours. If you do not have enough magic power, you must use more turns to bring more wizards to your empire.
gnome.era: As a Gnome, you are best suited for the Future Era. You can change eras by using the Advance to Present spell and then the Advance to Future Spell. Be sure you have enough magic power and mana to cast these spells. It may take a while to build up the resources needed to change eras. You must also wait 72 hours after changing eras to change again.
gnome.conclusion: You have now seen the basics of a cashing Gnome Empire. Your turns are best used gaining land, building huts and markets, and buying troops from the markets. Once you have exited new player protection, go to the War Council for a tutorial on attacking other players.

vampire.welcome: Welcome new Vampire leader! Vampires are able to perform a hybrid strategy between cash or troops production. This tutorial will show you the steps to get started with your empire.
vampire.land: Every empire needs as much land as they can get. While under new player protection, your only option to get more land is exploring. Click Explore in the sidebar.
vampire.explore: Explore for around 100 turns to get a base amount of land.
vampire.build: Now that you have land, you need to build buildings. Click Build in the sidebar.
vampire.strategy: As a Vampire, you can decide if you will focus more on cash or troop production to sell the excess on the Public Market. You should divide your land 30% huts, 30% markets, 35% blacksmiths and 5% mage towers. Mage towers will bring wizards to your land that will provide you with magical defense and the ability to change eras. Build these buildings until all of your land is used.
vampire.production: Now you should have a good base of cash and troops production. If you find yourself running low on food, you can buy food immediately on the Black Market but prices are usually better on the Public Market. For this tutorial we will start making cash. Click Cash in the sidebar.
vampire.cashing: Cash in batches of 25 turns and note the money you are producing and the expenses you are spending. You can take a more advanced approach to cashing by manipulating your tax rate. It is beneficial to lower your tax rate to 1% for a few turns to bring peasants into your empire, and then raise your tax rate to 100% for a few turns to get the most money out of them. Experiment with different tax rates to find the best balance for your empire.
vampire.banking: You should now have an excess of cash available in your empire. Click The Bank in the sidebar.
vampire.market: Deposit money in the Bank to earn interest. You can also take out loans if you need some quick cash. Now its time to buy things from the markets. Click Black Market in the sidebar.
vampire.blackmarket: Take note of the prices of items available on the Black Market. Click Public Market in the sidebar.
vampire.publicmarket: Take note of the prices of items available on the Public Market. Compare between the two to find the best deals. Click the arrow to continue the tutorial
vampire.magic: Now it is time to protect yourself from magic attacks. Click Magic Center in the sidebar.
vampire.shield: Check your magic power and mana supply. If you have a high enough magic power and enough mana, cast a Spell Shield. This will protect you from magical attacks for 24 hours. If you do not have enough magic power, you must use more turns to bring more wizards to your empire.
vampire.era: As a Vampire, you are suited for the Future Era. You can change eras by using the Advance to Present spell and then the Advance to Future Spell. Be sure you have enough magic power and mana to cast these spells. It may take a while to build up the resources needed to change eras. You must also wait 72 hours after changing eras to change again.
vampire.conclusion: You have now seen the basics of a Vampire Empire. Your turns are best used gaining land, building primarily huts and markets or blacksmiths and selling troops. Use the money you make to support your army's expenses and food consumption. Once you have exited new player protection, go to the War Council for a tutorial on attacking other players.

minotaur.production: Now you should have a good base of cash production. If you find yourself running low on food, you can buy food immediately on the Black Market but prices are usually better on the Public Market. Now we will start making more cash. Click Cash in the sidebar.
minotaur.cashing: Cash in batches of 25 turns and note the money you are producing and the expenses you are spending. You can take a more advanced approach to cashing by manipulating your tax rate. It is beneficial to lower your tax rate to 1% for a few turns to bring peasants into your empire, and then raise your tax rate to 100% for a few turns to get the most money out of them. Experiment with different tax rates to find the best balance for your empire.
minotaur.banking: You should now have an excess of cash available in your empire. Click The Bank in the sidebar.
minotaur.market: Deposit money in the Bank to earn interest. You can also take out loans if you need some quick cash. Now its time to buy things from the markets. Click Black Market in the sidebar.
minotaur.blackmarket: Take note of the prices of items available on the Black Market. Click Public Market in the sidebar.
minotaur.publicmarket: Take note of the prices of items available on the Public Market. Compare between the two to find the best deals. Click the arrow to continue the tutorial
minotaur.magic: Now it is time to protect yourself from magic attacks. Click Magic Center in the sidebar.
minotaur.shield: Check your magic power and mana supply. If you have a high enough magic power and enough mana, cast a Spell Shield. This will protect you from magical attacks for 24 hours. If you do not have enough magic power, you must use more turns to bring more wizards to your empire.
minotaur.era: As a Minotaur, you are best suited for the Future Era. You can change eras by using the Advance to Present spell and then the Advance to Future Spell. Be sure you have enough magic power and mana to cast these spells. It may take a while to build up the resources needed to change eras. You must also wait 72 hours after changing eras to change again.
minotaur.conclusion: You have now seen the basics of a cashing Minotaur Empire. Your turns are best used gaining land, building huts and markets, and buying troops from the markets. Once you have exited new player protection, go to the War Council for a tutorial on attacking other players.
